info.title: SCRB AI Crime Intelligence Platform
info.description: FastAPI Backend for SCRB Karnataka AI Crime Intelligence Platform

parameters.name: top_k

schema.description: Number of hotspot predictions to return
schema.title: Top K

parameters.description: Number of hotspot predictions to return

parameters.name: top_k

schema.description: Number of station risk rows to return
schema.title: Top K

parameters.description: Number of station risk rows to return

get.description:
Return a paginated list of FIR cases with optional filters.
Supports filtering by district, unit, status, and crime classification.

post.description:
Register a new FIR case.
Returns HTTP 409 if a case with the same FIR number already exists.

get.description: Return full case details for the given FIR number.
Return full case details including accused, victims, and arrests.

patch.description: Update the status of an existing case (e.g., mark as Chargesheeted).

get.description:
Return high-level KPIs for the main dashboard:
total cases, accused, victims, arrests, absconding suspects,
and cases currently under investigation.

schema.title: Response Dashboard Summary Analytics Dashboard Get

get.description:
Return case registrations grouped by month.
Optionally filter by year. Returns chronologically sorted data
suitable for line charts.

parameters.name: year

schema.description: Filter trends to a specific year
schema.title: Year

parameters.description: Filter trends to a specific year

schema.title: Response Crime Trends Analytics Crime Trends Get

get.description:
Return month-wise case volume with investigation and clearance breakdown.
Suitable for stacked charts and monthly operational reporting.

parameters.name: year

schema.description: Filter statistics to a specific year
schema.title: Year

parameters.description: Filter statistics to a specific year

schema.title: Response Monthly Statistics Analytics Monthly Statistics Get

get.description: Return total cases registered per calendar year.

schema.title: Response Yearly Summary Analytics Yearly Summary Get

get.description:
Return case counts grouped by crime category.
Optionally drill down into sub-categories by providing crime_head_id.
Suitable for pie/donut and bar charts.

parameters.name: crime_head_id

schema.description: Filter to subheads of a specific crime category
schema.title: Crime Head Id

parameters.description: Filter to subheads of a specific crime category

schema.title: Response Crime Type Distribution Analytics Crime Types Get

get.description:
Return crime hotspot data at district and station level.
If district is provided, returns police-station-level breakdown within it.
Drives the geographic heatmap on the dashboard.

parameters.name: district

schema.description: Drill into a specific district's police stations
schema.title: District

parameters.description: Drill into a specific district's police stations

get.description: Return police-station level workload statistics for operational dashboards.

schema.title: Response Police Station Statistics Analytics Police Stations Get

get.description: Return case counts grouped by investigation status.

schema.title: Response Case Status Analytics Status Get

get.description: Return accused counts grouped by their current status (Arrested, Absconding, etc.).

schema.title: Response Accused Status Analytics Accused Status Get

get.description:
Return the complete Case â Accused criminal network graph as
Cytoscape.js compatible JSON (nodes + edges + metadata).
Note: For large databases this may be slow; filtered endpoints are preferred.

Compute and return network centrality metrics:
- Degree centrality: nodes with the most connections
- Betweenness centrality: nodes acting as bridges between criminal clusters
- Top 10 most connected accused persons
- Top 10 most connected cases

schema.title: Response Network Metrics Network Metrics Get

get.description:
Return a localised network graph centred on the specified case,
including all accused and any other cases they are linked to.
Ideal for the investigation detail view.

schema.title: Response Case Network Network Case  Case Id  Get

get.description:
Return all cases and co-accused connected to a specific individual.
Useful for building a criminal profile and history.

schema.title: Response Accused Network Network Accused  Accused Id  Get

get.description: Return all accused persons currently marked as Absconding. Used for alert dashboards.

schema.title: Response Get Absconding Accused Absconding Get

get.description: Cross-case search for accused persons by name (case-insensitive partial match).

parameters.name: name

schema.description: Partial or full name to search
schema.title: Name

parameters.description: Partial or full name to search

schema.title: Response Search Accused Accused Search Get

get.description: Return all accused persons linked to the specified case.

schema.title: Response Get Accused For Case Cases  Case Id  Accused Get

post.description: Add a new accused person to an existing case.

get.description: Return all victims linked to the specified case.

schema.title: Response Get Victims For Case Cases  Case Id  Victims Get

post.description: Add a new victim to an existing case.

get.description:
Generate a comprehensive investigation report for a single FIR case.
Includes case metadata, crime classification, accused/victim lists,
arrest details, criminal network sub-graph, and an AI summary placeholder.

schema.title: Response Case Report Reports Case  Case Id  Get

get.description:
Generate a crime statistics summary report for an entire district.
Includes monthly trends, crime category breakdown, unit hotspots,
and KPI summaries. Optionally filter statistics to a specific year.

schema.title: Response District Report Reports District Get

get.description:
Generate the complete dashboard data payload in a single call.
Includes KPIs, trends, hotspots, crime type distribution,
case status breakdowns, and network metrics.

schema.title: Response Dashboard Report Reports Dashboard Get
